name: agent-confirm-sound
description: Plays a sound when a terminal-based AI agent (Claude Code, aider, etc.) is waiting for you to confirm or approve an action.

agentConfirmSound.enabled.description: Enable watching terminals for confirmation prompts.

agentConfirmSound.patterns.description: Case-insensitive regex patterns matched against terminal output. A sound plays on the first match (then debounces). Tune these for the agents you use — check the output channel to see what's actually coming through.

agentConfirmSound.terminalNameFilter.description: Optional. If non-empty, only terminals whose name contains one of these strings (case-insensitive) are watched. Leave empty to watch all terminals. Example: ["claude", "aider"]

agentConfirmSound.soundPath.description: Optional absolute path to a custom sound file (.wav / .mp3 / .aiff). Leave empty to use the bundled default sound.

agentConfirmSound.debounceMs.description: Minimum time in milliseconds between sounds for the same terminal, to avoid repeated triggers on the same prompt.

agentConfirmSound.volume.description: Volume level from 0 (silent) to 1 (full). Applied via afplay on macOS and paplay on Linux. Windows SoundPlayer does not support volume control.

agentConfirmSound.focusTerminal.description: When a pattern matches, bring the matching terminal into focus automatically.

scripts.package: npm run compile && npx vsce package --out dist/
